name: Rainforest Alliance
mission: The Rainforest Alliance works to encourage better on-the-ground forestry practices by rewarding businesses, communities and governments that meet standards for sustainability.
theoryOfChange: The Rainforest Alliance has a published 'Theory of Change for the 2020 Certification Program' which outlines the results chain logic, including outputs, short-term outcomes, intermediate outcomes, long-term outcomes, and impacts. This framework is used for internal monitoring and evaluation and for communicating program impacts to external stakeholders. The Theory of Change reflects key features of their 'Reimagining Certification' approach, emphasizing continuous improvement and shared responsibility across the supply chain. Their monitoring and evaluation methodology explicitly states the use of a Theory of Change approach to design projects and programs with indicators to track outcomes and impacts.
scoreSummary: RAINFOREST ALLIANCE shows exceptional impact and good alignment.

evaluationSources: COSA evaluation of UTZ certification in Ghana
Institute of Social Development evaluation of tea program in Sri Lanka

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

components.evidence: Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.evidence: Niche cause: ADVOCACY (+4); Serves underserved populations (+3)
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 30/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; good program ratio (≥75%) suggests reasonable delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

score_details.score_summary: RAINFOREST ALLIANCE shows exceptional impact and good alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://give.rainforest-alliance.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $100,221,940 and a program expense ratio of 83.5%.
The organization generates significant royalty revenue from certified products to fund its sustainability programs.
The organization accepts general charitable donations (Sadaqah) to support its mission.
The organization holds a 96.5/100 score, holds 7.8 months of working capital, and has a fundraising efficiency of $0.04 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 74/100 due to its exceptional financial health and high Charity Navigator score of 96.5/100. While its impact model is focused on long-term advocacy and capacity building, its operational transparency is high.
areas_for_improvement: Strengthening direct impact measurement beyond capacity building and advocacy
Enhancing specific resonance with niche donor demographics

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a Sadaqah-eligible organization, it offers a medium fit for donors focused on environmental stewardship and ethical commerce [4]. The cause is categorized under advocacy, reflecting a long-term track record of influencing global supply chains and government standards [5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial accountability with a 96.96.5/100 from Charity Navigator and directs 83.5% of its expenses toward programs in forestry, agriculture, and livelihoods [1][5]. Its impact model is categorized as capacity building, focusing on systemic change through certification and sustainable practice rewards [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Rainforest Alliance fosters global sustainability by rewarding businesses and communities that implement ethical forestry and agricultural practices.
strengths: High financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 83.5%
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.04 to raise every $1
Robust liquidity with 7.8 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: The Rainforest Alliance operates as an international non-profit dedicated to protecting forests and improving the livelihoods of farmers and forest communities through market-based sustainability standards [1][5]. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $100,221,940 to support its global conservation and advocacy initiatives [1][2].
